name: SMITHFIELD FOODS INC
business: Smithfield Foods produces a wide variety of packaged meats and fresh pork products primarily in the United States. The company operates through three reportable segments: Packaged Meats, Fresh Pork, and Hog Production. Products are sold to retail, foodservice, industrial customers, and export markets.
sellsTo: foodservice industry including restaurants and hotel chains
export markets in China, Mexico, Japan, South Korea, Canada

exposures.sentence: We employ automation in our plants to redeploy labor to higher value tasks, improve yields and drive efficiency by reducing complexity.
We use automation in our distribution centers to pick and fulfill orders and thereby reduce labor costs.
by-products that were once considered waste are now used to create renewable natural gas through our biogas joint ventures and lifesaving pharmaceuticals through our bioscience business.
Our Bioscience operations use raw materials from hogs that we process to manufacture heparin products, including an active pharmaceutical ingredient that mitigates the risk of blood clots.

we will employ new and advanced technologies, including artificial intelligence and robotic process automation, that will allow us to drive significant improvements in operational efficiency and productivity.

catalysts.event: Construction of new Sioux Falls processing facility
catalysts.sentence: construction is anticipated to begin in the first half of 2027 with production estimated to commence by the end of 2028.
The closing of the transaction is expected to occur in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. pork product export volume was 3.5 million tons in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the USDA, total U.S. pork product export volume was 3.5 million tons in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. share of the global pork export market increased to 30% in 2025 from 2% in 1990.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), the U.S. share of the global pork export market increased to 30% in 2025 from 2% in 1990.
